data.feature_description: Input slicer to allow numeric data colors for filtering and forced numeric entry.
data.feature_name: Input slicer numeric support

data.feature_description: A new option in the Table and Matrix visuals to specify each columns width.
data.feature_name: Table and matrix fixed width columns

data.blog_title: Tenant level private link support for Microsoft Fabric API for GraphQL (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: Fabric Graph supports private connectivity using Fabric private endpoints and private links. This allows customers to access graph workloads over private networks, keep traffic off the public internet, and deploy Fabric Graph in locked down enterprise network environments. The service validates graph APIs under private access configurations and propagates the required access context for trusted service to service communication.
data.feature_name: Fabric Graph supports private network access for enterprise environments

data.blog_title: OneLake Security on the SQL Analytics Endpoint
data.feature_description: Fabric Graph conforms to OneLake security so that access controls defined over lake data are enforced consistently when data is represented and queried as a graph. Row , column , and object level policies apply end to end, preventing graphs from becoming an alternate access path. This alignment ensures customers can adopt Fabric Graph without introducing new security models or weakening existing governance and compliance controls.
data.feature_name: Fabric Graph enforces OneLake security for governed graph access

data.blog_title: Introducing Graph in Microsoft Fabric – Connected Data for the Era of AI
data.feature_description: Fabric Graph integrates with Fabric Data Agent and supports NL2GQL, enabling users to ask questions about graph data in natural language. Queries are translated into GQL, executed against the graph, and returned as grounded results. Users can inspect the executed query or navigate back to the graph experience for deeper exploration. This capability allows Fabric Graph to participate directly in agent driven and Copilot scenarios while maintaining transparency and trust.
data.feature_name: Fabric Graph makes connected data accessible through natural language data agents

data.feature_description: Fabric Graph increases GQL feature coverage to support richer relationship modeling and investigation scenarios. This includes dynamic nested data, OPTIONAL MATCH, extended FOR semantics (IS SOURCE OF, IS DESTINATION OF, COLLECT ONE), result shaping functions (NODES, EDGES, INNER NODES), quantified simple edge patterns, and shortest path queries.
data.feature_name: Fabric Graph expands GQL support for real world relationship queries

data.blog_title: Simplifying Data Ingestion with Copy job – Introducing Change Data Capture (CDC) Support (Preview)
data.feature_description: The Change Data Capture (CDC) capability in Copy Job is generally available with Azure SQL DB, SQL Server and SQL MI
data.feature_name: Copy job -  Change Data Capture (CDC) capability GA with SQL estates

data.feature_description: Always make sure your report viewers land on the right page
data.feature_name: Set as landing page in Power BI reports

data.feature_description: A visual calculation is a DAX calculation that's defined and executed directly on a visual. Visual calculations make it easier to create calculations previously hard to create, right in the visual.
data.feature_name: Visual calculations GA

data.blog_title: Fabric Extensibility Toolkit: Publishing Workloads announcements
data.feature_description: The Fabric Extensibility Toolkit (FET) now provides migration support from the Workload Development Kit (WDK), enabling developers to transition existing workloads to the modern FET platform with minimal friction. This capability ensures continuity for solutions built on WDK while unlocking advanced features. Migration guidance includes documentation, and best practices to streamline the process -- from updating manifest versions to adopting new APIs and SDKs.
data.feature_name: Fabric Extensibility Toolkit - Workload Development Kit Migration support

data.blog_title: Fabric Extensibility Toolkit: Publishing Workloads announcements
data.feature_description: The Fabric Extensibility Toolkit (FET) will offer comprehensive CI/CD support for all Fabric items build with the Framework. This will address a main feedback from customers that want to leverage for all their items that are part of a workspace.
data.feature_name: Fabric Extensibility Toolkit - CI/CD support for all items

data.blog_title: Fabric CLI: explore and automate Microsoft Fabric from your terminal (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: Key capabilities of fabric-cicd library are now integrated directly into the Fabric CLI. With the new deploy command, you can automate full workspace deployments -- including item rebinding and configuration -- from the command line. This unifies your deployment workflows and reduces the need for custom scripts or additional tools.
data.feature_name: Fabric CI/CD in the CLI

data.blog_title: Introducing Fabric MCP (Preview)
data.feature_description: We're launching the public preview of Fabric Remote MCP -- a secure, cloud-hosted execution engine that lets AI agents and automation tools perform authenticated actions in Fabric using a standardized protocol. This eliminates the need for custom integrations and enables seamless automation of tasks like workspace provisioning and item deployment. It's a major step toward an AI-native Fabric experience.
data.feature_name: Fabric Remote MCP (Public Preview)

data.blog_title: Fabric Extensibility—Self Service Workload Publishing (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: **Workload Self-Service Publishing to selected audience**ISVs can now self-service publish workloads built with the [Fabric Extensibility Toolkit](https://learn.microsoft.com/fabric/extensibility-toolkit/extensibility-toolkit-overview) into Selected Audience (private preview). This capability allows publishers to reserve a WorkloadName directly by uploading their manifest through the Fabric Admin Portal--removing the previous dependency on the Microsoft team to pre-register the name.In addition, publishers can now publish their workloads to up to 20 external tenants for early testing and validation.**Key Capabilities**Self-service registration - Reserve a workload ID and publish workloads end-to-end through the portal.
data.feature_name: Fabric Extensibility Toolkit - Workload Self-Service Publishing (Selected audience)

data.blog_title: Introducing the Microsoft Fabric Extensibility Toolkit
data.feature_description: Items built with the Fabric Extensibility Toolkit can now pick up and resolve Variables, allowing them to dynamically adapt to the context in which they run. When a user opens an Item, the Item automatically evaluates any referenced Variables--such as workspace metadata, user-provided parameters, or environment-specific values--and replaces them with their resolved results. This enables Items to behave consistently across different workspaces and scenarios, while still responding to variable-driven configuration.
data.feature_name: Fabric Extensibility Toolkit - Variable support

data.blog_title: Fabric Extensibility Toolkit: Publishing Workloads announcements
data.feature_description: The Fabric Extensibility Toolkit (FET) now supports CRUD Notifications, achieving full parity with the Workload Development Kit (WDK) and closing a critical gap by adding remote job capabilities. This enhancement enables developers to manage create, read, update, and delete operations with real-time notifications while orchestrating remote execution scenarios seamlessly. By aligning with WDK's lifecycle management and introducing remote job handling, FET empowers teams to build responsive, distributed solutions across Fabric workspaces without additional backend complexity.
data.feature_name: Fabric Extensibility Toolkit - Workload Development Kit parity

data.blog_title: Fabric Extensibility Toolkit: Publishing Workloads announcements
data.feature_description: The Fabric Extensibility Framework (FET) is officially General Availability (GA), marking a major milestone in empowering developers to build, customize, and extend Fabric experiences at scale. GA brings enterprise-grade stability. With comprehensive documentation, standardized UX controls, and robust governance support, FET enables partners and developers to deliver secure, performant, and compliant solutions confidently across the Fabric ecosystem.
data.feature_name: Fabric Extensibility Toolkit - General Availability

data.blog_title: Introducing Fabric MCP (Preview)
data.feature_description: After a successful public preview, Fabric Local MCP is now generally available. This open-source MCP server runs locally on your machine, connecting AI coding assistants like GitHub Copilot to Fabric. It provides API schemas and validation, item definitions, and other resources to help developers generate accurate code and streamline local-to-cloud workflows -- now with improved tooling and broader capabilities.
data.feature_name: Fabric Local MCP (General Availability)

data.blog_title: New in Microsoft Fabric: Empowering Workspace Admins with Direct Workload Assignment
data.feature_description: Workload Management Admin API - Public PreviewTenant administrators can now manage extensibility workloads using the new Workload Management Admin API.Key CapabilitiesList workloads - List all available extensibility workloads for assignmentList assignments - View current workload assignments in the tenant across scopesAdd workload assignment - Assign workloads at tenant, capacity, or workspace scopeDelete workload assignment - Remove workload assignments
data.feature_name: Fabric Extensibility Toolkit - Workload Management Admin API

data.blog_title: New in Microsoft Fabric: Empowering Workspace Admins with Direct Workload Assignment
data.feature_description: Introducing Workload Management in the Fabric Admin PortalTenant administrators can now discover and manage Extensibility workloads from a single, centralized experience in the Admin Portal.What's NewManage tab with searchable, filterable workloads tableEach workload has a detail panel for inspection and action, which includes:Consent - Pre-consent the entire tenantManage workload availability in the tenant by capacities and workspaces
data.feature_name: Fabric Extensibility Toolkit - Admin Portal Workload Managment

data.blog_title: Announcing general availability of the Fabric Workload Development Kit
data.feature_description: Python SDK GA alllows to build, automate, and integrate with Microsoft Fabric faster.With GA stability and support, developers and ISVs can programmatically manage Fabric items, workspaces, and operations using clean, idiomatic Python APIs--designed for production-scale automation, CI/CD, and embedded experiences.
data.feature_name: Fabric Python SDK GA

data.blog_title: New in Microsoft Fabric: Empowering Workspace Admins with Direct Workload Assignment
data.feature_description: Workspace-Level Workload Assignment - Now Generally AvailableWe're excited to announce that workspace-level workload assignment is now generally available in Microsoft Fabric.First introduced in Public Preview, this capability empowers workspace admins to add extensibility workloads directly to their workspace.Key CapabilitiesWorkspace admins can open the Workload Hub and add workloads directly to their workspace, no capacity-level permissions requiredAfter the workload is added, workspace users can consume the new workload items
data.feature_name: Fabric Extensibility Toolkit - Workspace-Level Workload Assignment GA

data.blog_title: Microsoft Fabric extension for VS Code (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: The Microsoft Fabric ADO Pipelines Extension bundles the Fabric CLI and enables automated workspace creation and deployment of Fabric items in Azure Pipelines, ensuring consistent and repeatable delivery across environments.
data.feature_name: ADO Pipelines Extension for MS Fabric

data.blog_title: Easily load Fabric OneLake data into Excel — OneLake catalog and Get Data are integrated into Excel for Windows (Preview)
data.feature_description: The OneLake catalog integration in Excel as part of modern Get Data unlocks seamless connectivity to Fabric data, empowering every business user to discover and analyze organizational content directly from Excel. This powerful capability simplifies data access and accelerates insights, making Excel a true gateway to Fabric's rich data ecosystem.
data.feature_name: Fabric OneLake catalog in Excel Get Data

data.blog_title: Fabric Real-Time Analytics Integrates with Newly Announced Database Watcher for Azure SQL
data.feature_description: Integration on Fabric SQL DB in Workspace Monitoring.
data.feature_name: Fabric SQL DB in Workspace Monitoring

data.blog_title: Fabric IQ: The Semantic Foundation for Enterprise AI
data.feature_description: Synthetic AI engineers for Industrial Operations that help users automate routine tasks so human engineers can bring their ingenuity to creative and complex challenges.Its industrial semantics, ontologies, physics aware models and purpose built agents supply the understanding that turns raw data into  industrial grade operational intelligence using RTI on Fabric as the backbone. Eventstreams capture and transform data at massive scale, eventhouse delivers fast and flexible analytics and OneLake ensures that context is unified and discoverable.
data.feature_name: Intuigence AI

data.blog_title: Introducing Graph in Microsoft Fabric – Connected Data for the Era of AI
data.feature_description: Fabric Graph is designed to operate at very large scale, supporting graphs with billions of elements while maintaining predictable performance and reliability. Ongoing investments improve query stability, ingestion throughput, and service resiliency. Operational visibility is enhanced by exposing detailed ingestion failure information and clearer diagnostic signals, enabling customers to identify, understand, and resolve issues more quickly as graph workloads scale into production.
data.feature_name: Fabric Graph delivers reliable performance at billion scale

data.feature_description: This feature allows users to create property definitions without immediately selecting a data type or binding to a data source. Users can define high-level business concepts first and assign data types later when real data is bound, at which point the entity type updates automatically. This flexibility lets users model concepts early without needing to know data types or source details upfront.
data.feature_name: Modeling Untyped Properties

data.blog_title: Announcing backup storage billing for SQL database in Microsoft Fabric: what you need to know
data.feature_description: Users should be able to restore a database from backups of a deleted database in their workspaces.
data.feature_name: Fabric SQL - Restore of deleted databases

data.blog_title: Industrial Analytics delivered at-scale: Powered by Fabric Real-Time Intelligence and Fusion Data Hub
data.feature_description: Fusion Data Hub, utilizing Fabric Real-Time Intelligence seamlessly moves your operational (OT) data, including near real time streaming, historical, metadata and hierarchical data from Historian systems and similar into Microsoft Fabric Onelake and prepares your data for Industrial Analytics and AI transformations.Using Fusion's solution on Fabric customers can ingest their OT(Operational) data from on-prem or the Cloud into Fabric and unify with their IT data stored in ERP, MES, SCM and other business systems all in OneLake to unlock actionable insights supporting a wide range of personas from engineers to executives in the organization.Key use-cases"1. Visualizations via Fusion or PowerBI2. Industrial analytics3. AI/ML with ready to use data in OneLake.
data.feature_name: Fusion Data Hub

data.blog_title: Unlocking financial insights with Capital Markets DataHub workload—A partner-led innovation in Microsoft Fabric (Preview)
data.feature_description: Financial Fabric provides a comprehensive set of data assets from 200+ sources to help fund managers, institutional investors and others in capital markets make smarter decisions quickly.  With Financial Fabric in Microsoft Fabric, users can easily brownse and consume financial data directly in Fabric Warehouse, Power BI or operationalize it using Data Agents and Copilot. There are 3 key features the workload offers:1. Data Discovery - Offers a browsable catalog of 200 + data assets from Capital Market data providers organized using a detailed financial taxonomy.2. Data Delivery - Offers normalized canonical models that are secure, governed and meet financial services regulatory compliance, so it is AI ready.3. Data Interaction - With data in OneLake users can access the data via Excel, Fabric Notebooks, Power BI or Datahub Chatbot experience.
data.feature_name: Financial Fabric - Capital Markets Data Hub

data.blog_title: OneLake Security on the SQL Analytics Endpoint
data.feature_description: General availability of OneLake security's core features: folder, table, row, and column level security across all Fabric workloads.
data.feature_name: OneLake security GA

data.blog_title: How Stibo Systems’ MDM powers trusted data for analytics and AI in Microsoft Fabric (Preview)
data.feature_description: Stibo's **DaaS workload **exposes its multi-domain Master Data Management (MDM) capabilities as a service within Microsoft Fabric, enabling customers to consume trusted master data directly inside their analytics and AI workflows. This includes product, customer, supplier, and other core enterprise domains, delivered as governed, curated datasets rather than raw exports.Product Features1. Fabric-Native Data Access via OneLake - Customers can materialize governed master data in OneLake and use the data for both BI and AI use-cases2.Configurable Data Export & Synchronization - Ability to select specific master data, denormalize data, managaging update frequecy enabling self-service data provisioning which preserving the governance model in the MDM system3.AI enriched master data - Automated product description generation, intelligent data quality recommendations and corrections. 4. Unified Analytics via Power BI - Leveraging the full Power BI co-pilot capabilities.
data.feature_name: Stibo Master Data Management

data.blog_title: Custom SQL Pools for Fabric Data Warehouse (Preview)
data.feature_description: Custom SQL Pools will provide user managed workload isolation boundaries as well as the ability to control the burstable capacity limit.
data.feature_name: Custom SQL Pools

data.blog_title: Evaluate Power Query Programmatically in Microsoft Fabric (Preview)
data.feature_description: Modern Get Data offers highly intuitive methods for users to connect to their data from a wide range of data sources. By integrating Modern Get Data with Power BI Desktop, users will also be able to easily discover and consume data stored in Fabric via the OneLake Catalog which is now deeply integrated within the Modern Get Data experience. This Modern Get Data experience facilitates an efficient process for importing data into Power BI reports to enable self-service data analytics.
data.feature_name: Power Query - Modern Power Query Get Data in Power BI Desktop

data.blog_title: Enhancing Data Transformation Flexibility with Multiple-Schema Inferencing in Eventstream (Preview)
data.feature_description: The Enhanced Eventstream UX for non-schematized events GA feature supports differentianting shapes from various sources and eventstream itself, enabling you to design different data transformation paths by picking up one of the shapes with rich flexibility. This allows for seamless data integration and processing, catering to complex and multiple data shapes environment
data.feature_name: Enhanced Eventstream UX for non-schematized events GA

data.feature_description: The SQL database definition includes the objects that reside in the database, but the multi-dimensional nature of a database results in a requirement for additional actions during deployment. SQL database projects include a feature for incorporating open-ended T-SQL scripts for execution before and after the database deployment. Pre-deployment and post-deployment scripts unlock data cleaning before deployment, reference data management, and other advanced scenarios for CI/CD.
data.feature_name: Pre-/post-deployment scripts in CI/CD

data.blog_title: SQL Projects support for Warehouse in Microsoft Fabric
data.feature_description: The REST APIs for SQL database in Fabric are being extended to support the full SQL project file format in addition to the existing dacpac format. These APIs enable you to retrieve the code definition of your databases or have your database dynamically updated based on a code definition that you provide.- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/fabric/sqldatabase/items/get-sql-database-definition?tabs=HTTP- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/fabric/sqldatabase/items/update-sql-databases-definition?tabs=HTTPLearn more about SQL projects from https://aka.ms/sqlprojects
data.feature_name: SQL project database definition import/export API

data.feature_description: Fabric Data Warehouse will introduce T-SQL functions for pattern matching, text extraction, and transformation using regular expressions. These capabilities will make it easier to validate, search, and manipulate text data directly within your queries.New functions include:* REGEXP_LIKE - Returns a Boolean indicating if the text matches the regex pattern.* REGEXP_REPLACE - Replaces occurrences of a regex pattern with a specified string.* REGEXP_SUBSTR - Extracts parts of a string based on a regex pattern, including Nth occurrence.* REGEXP_INSTR - Returns the position (start or end) of a matched substring.* REGEXP_COUNT - Counts how many times a regex pattern occurs in a string.* REGEXP_MATCHES - Returns a table of captured substrings matching the regex pattern.* REGEXP_SPLIT_TO_TABLE - Splits a string into rows using a regex delimiter.
data.feature_name: Regular expressions

data.feature_description: Fabric Data Warehouse will introduce string similarity and comparison functions based on Levenshtein and Jaro-Winkler algorithms. These functions make it easier to find strings that are similar, even when they have small changes or spelling errors.New functions that will be added are:* EDIT_DISTANCE - Returns the number of edits (insertions, deletions, substitutions) needed to transform one string into another.* EDIT_DISTANCE_SIMILARITY - Calculates a similarity score (0-1) based on Levenshtein distance, where 1 means identical strings.* JARO_WINKLER_DISTANCE - Measures the distance between two strings using the Jaro-Winkler algorithm, considering transpositions and common prefixes.* JARO_WINKLER_SIMILARITY - Returns a similarity score (0-1) using Jaro-Winkler, optimized for short strings and minor typos.
data.feature_name: Fuzzy string matching

data.blog_title: Announcing Shortcut Transformations: from files to Delta tables. Always in sync, no pipelines required.
data.feature_description: With the upcoming enhancements to Shortcut Transformations, you'll be able to fully customize how you bring in data from CSV, Parquet, JSON, and Excel files. You can set file encoding, choose custom quote or escape characters for your data in case of CSV files. For CSV, Parquet and JSON files, you'll have options to decide refresh mode (Append Only or Mirror) and schema mode (Dynamic or Fixed).  All these controls will be available directly in the user interface, giving you more flexibility, reliability, and efficiency in managing your data ingestion and transformation workflows.
data.feature_name: Shortcut Transformations - Customize ingestion with user configurations

data.blog_title: Simplifying Medallion Implementation with Materialized Lake Views in Fabric
data.feature_description: Multi Lakehouse support in Lineage enables users to visualize and manage dependencies of Materialized Lake Views (MLVs) across multiple workspaces and lakehouses, providing a unified view that helps prevent data silos and improves transparency. It is designed to support scalable lineage tracking, advanced search, and focused navigation, making it easier for data teams to trace upstream and downstream dependencies.
data.feature_name: Fabric Materialized Lake Views - Multi Workspace/Lakehouse execution support
Outbound Access Protection for EventHouse

data.blog_title: Updates to default data destination behavior in Dataflow Gen2
data.feature_description: A commonly requested new capability for Output Destinations is the ability to merge, or upsert, data into previously loaded rows in the destination table. We aim to provide this support for Fabric Lakehouse destination.
data.feature_name: Dataflows - Merge/Upsert Support for Output Destinations

data.blog_title: Introducing SQL Audit Logs for Fabric Data Warehouse
data.feature_description: This feature will introduce enhancements to SQL Audit Logs, including support for audit event filtering and query size enforcement.These capabilities help customers reduce audit noise and improve audit completeness by controlling which events are captured and preventing oversized queries from exceeding audit limits.
data.feature_name: SQL Audit Logs Improvments

data.feature_description: The scaled canvas UX focuses on delivering a more consistent and scalable UX that supports advanced ontology configurations. Current workflows are fragmented and limit discoverability, making it harder for users to manage ontologies effectively. The scaled design features unified navigation, scalable and expandable elements, and better interaction patterns for clearer entities, relationships, rules, and more. This approach is grounded in user research, where participants responded positively to the navigation and ontology visualization, with feedback on specific pain points incorporated into iterative improvements.
data.feature_name: Scaled Configuration Canvas

data.feature_description: Oracle connector supports cloud-based scenario by bundling the Oracle Data Provider NET driver and avoiding users having to use an On-premises data gateway for Cloud-only scenarios.
data.feature_name: Connectors - Oracle Connector support without requiring a gateway

data.blog_title: Fabric Data Pipelines – Advanced Scheduling Techniques (Part 2: Run a Pipeline on a Specific Day)
data.feature_description: Pipelines in Fabric Data Factory emphasize general re-use patterns and metadata driven methodologies. Now with support for pipeline parameters in schedules you can create multiple schedules per pipeline with different parameter values enabling incredibly powerful generic pipeline workflow patterns.
data.feature_name: Pipelines - Support pipeline parameters in schedules

data.blog_title: Run Spark Job Definitions in Pipelines with Service Principal or Workspace Identity
data.feature_description: When automating your Fabric Data Factory pipelines, you will greatly benefit from having the flexibility to set the identity (i.e. user, SPN, workspace identity) of the pipeine at the time of execution. With this feature, you can now set it easily from the scheduler API and scheduler UI.
data.feature_name: Pipelines - Set the "Run As" identity of the pipeline from the pipeline schedule

data.blog_title: BULK INSERT in Fabric Data Warehouse
data.feature_description: Fabric Data Warehouse will support the bcp utility and the TDS Bulk Load API, enabling high-performance data ingestion from a variety of client tools such as bcp, SSIS, and Azure Data Factory. This integration simplifies bulk data loading into Fabric DW and supports scalable, efficient workflows. Centralized support for these APIs ensures consistency across ingestion pipelines and improves interoperability with existing tools.<br/>An example of a bcp command that loads file content into a DW table:<br/>```bcp dbo.artists in gold_artist.txt -d TextDW -c -S myworkspace.datawarehouse.fabric.microsoft.com -G -U theuser@microsoft.com ```
data.feature_name: BCP

data.feature_description: The EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE feature allows you to define a root URI that can be referenced by name in OPENROWSET and BULK INSERT operations. By using the DATA SOURCE option, you can reference files using relative paths, eliminating the need to specify long absolute URIs.
data.feature_name: External data sources

data.blog_title: Inline Scalar user-defined functions (UDFs) in Microsoft Fabric Warehouse (Preview)
data.feature_description: Computation-based scalar UDFs now support WHILE loops, multiple RETURN statements and deeply nested IF-THEN-ELSE blocks in the function body. They can also be used in queries that include Common Table Expressions (CTEs), and within GROUP BY, HAVING, and ORDER BY clauses.
data.feature_name: Scalar UDFs - Expanded T-SQL constructs and query shapes for computation-based UDFs

data.blog_title: Task flows in Microsoft Fabric (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: The multiple task flows in a workspace feature enables users to create, manage, and navigate multiple independent task flows within the same workspace. Each task flow has its own isolated canvas, clear identity, and lifecycle, allowing teams to organize work by project or process without impacting others.
data.feature_name: Multiple task flows in a workspace

data.blog_title: Announcing GitHub integration for source control (Preview)
data.feature_description: Ability to connect Fabric workspace to repositories which reside on GitHub Enterprise Cloud with data residency
data.feature_name: Git Integration provider - GitHub Enterprise Cloud with data residency support

data.blog_title: Optimize your storage costs with OneLake storage tiers and lifecycle management (Preview)
data.feature_description: OneLake provides rule-based lifecycle management to help organizations optimize storage costs. These policies allow administrators to automatically transition data between hot, cool, and cold tiers based on last access or modification time.
data.feature_name: OneLake Storage Lifecycle Management Policies

data.feature_description: Quickly identify when and where throttling is occurring across your Eventhouse system. This feature highlights recent throttling events, helping you diagnose performance bottlenecks and take corrective action before they impact users.
data.feature_name: show Throttling events of eventhouse at Eventhouse WS monitoring

data.feature_description: A common scenario for Dataflow Gen2 customers in Fabric is wanting to reference items in the "current workspace" rather than specifically referencing items in a given workspace. This allows scenarios such as deploying Dataflow Gen2 (CI/CD) items across workspaces, and have those read or write data to other Fabric artifacts (such as Lakehouse, Warehouse, etc.).As part of this planned item, we will introduce the ability for users to select an item in "current workspace" as part of the overall navigation experience for Fabric sources/destinations in Dataflow Gen2, instead of it being hardcoded to an specific workspace.
data.feature_name: Dataflows - Relative references to Fabric items within the "current workspace"

data.blog_title: Announcing Cosmos DB in Microsoft Fabric Featuring New Capabilities! (Preview)
data.feature_description: Cosmos DB in Fabric allows customers to build operational and analytical workloads in Microsoft Fabric. Leverage the AI-features available in Cosmos DB and take advantage of the innovations in Fabric with SQL queries over JSON data, build real-time intelligence over transactional data, use Cosmos DB as a lightning fast serving layer for Power BI with reverse ETL using Spark, as well as Copilot-powered Microsoft Power BI, -- all without having to stitch together separate services. Cosmos DB in Fabric now includes many new features. For example, build User-Data Functions that connect seamlessly with your Cosmos DB artifacts, use continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) to manage development, test, and production pipelines. This release leverages Cosmos DB's autoscale for elasticity when you need it, and vector and full-text indexing and search enabled by default to support your AI-enabled workloads. This is a major step toward making operational data readily accessible across the Fabric ecosystem.
data.feature_name: Cosmos DB in Fabric

data.blog_title: Cosmos DB in Microsoft Fabric and Cosmos DB Mirroring (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: Now generally available, Azure Cosmos DB Mirroring in Fabric allows you to seamlessly integrate your existing operational workloads with the analytical capabilities of Microsoft Fabric. Take advantage of the innovations in Fabric with SQL queries over JSON data and build real-time intelligence over your existing transactional data. Integrate with the Microsoft Fabric ecosystem of services, as well as Copilot-powered Microsoft Power BI, -- all without having to stitch together separate services. Azure Cosmos DB Mirroring in Fabric allows you to select which containers to mirror into Fabric, giving you complete control over what data you build analytics on. Get the best of both worlds leveraging the SLA-backed latency and availability you have come to expect from Azure Cosmos DB with Microsoft Fabric's array of analytical services and features, making it even easier now to bring your existing operational data and make accessible across the Fabric ecosystem.
data.feature_name: Azure Cosmos DB Mirroring in Microsoft Fabric

data.feature_description: A current limitation is that we do not present users with the name of the artifact that produced an artifact execution, for example, when a Data Pipeline triggers the execution of a Dataflow Gen2 artifact. We will provide a TriggeredBy field to capture the origin of each item invocation for monitoring purposes.
data.feature_name: Data Pipelines - "Triggered By" information for Data Pipeline activities

data.blog_title: Entity Diagram in Eventhouse KQL Database (Preview)
data.feature_description: Enable users to view the Eventstream that streams data into their KQL DB table (direct or indirect mode), directly from the DB Entity Diagram UI.
data.feature_name: [KQL DB] Entities diagram ("lineage") - include Evenstreams in the diagram

data.blog_title: Unlock the Power of Real-Time Intelligence in Fabric: Connect and stream events effortlessly with the Get events experience (preview)!
data.feature_description: From within an activator item, you choose 'Get data' to pull events from Real-Time Hub. You get search and catalog capabilities over all streams in your organization, and can connect to external sources with connectors to streams, change data capture feeds and more.
data.feature_name: "Get Events" from Activator

data.blog_title: Fabric Eventstream SQL Operator: Your tool kit to Real-Time data processing in Fabric Real-Time Intelligence
data.feature_description: Customers are able to get event data from eventstreams in multiple Fabric items, including Lakehouse, KQL Database, and Reflex.
data.feature_name: "Get data from Eventstream" in multiple Fabric items

data.feature_description: Currently the largest message size Eventstream can support is 1MB. This new feature will push this limit to bigger size to meet broader requirements.
data.feature_name: Eventstream supports large message size

data.blog_title: Acquiring Real-Time Data from New Sources with Enhanced Eventstream
data.feature_description: The Reference Data Join feature in Eventstream enables you to enrich streaming events by joining them with static or slowly changing delta tables in one lake.User can reference any one lake delta tables using lakehouse, and join it with streaming data in eventstream to enahace and add additional context to their events during processing.
data.feature_name: Eventstream reference data join with one lake delta tables

data.blog_title: New Eventstream sources: MQTT, Solace PubSub+, Azure Data Explorer, Weather & Azure Event Grid
data.feature_description: This feature enables customers to develop or re-use their own Kafka connectors or open-source Kafka connectors on Fabric when there are no pre-built streaming connectors available.
data.feature_name: Eventstream supports customers to upload own connector

data.blog_title: Simplifying Data Ingestion with Copy job – Incremental Copy GA, Lakehouse Upserts, and New Connectors
data.feature_description: The integration of Real-Time Intelligence with CopyJob in Microsoft Fabric empowers organizations to stream incremental updates from traditionally 'batch' data sources directly into Eventstreams. Simultaneously, users can ingest data from streaming sources into any CopyJob destination that supports incremental updates. This unified experience makes it easy to build hybrid data platforms that combine batch and streaming assets. As a result, creating event-driven and AI-powered applications--like dashboards, alerts, and compliance solutions--no longer requires manual data movement or complex integrations.
data.feature_name: Unify batch & streaming data platforms with CopyJob & Real-Time Intelligence

data.blog_title: Mirroring for SQL Server in Microsoft Fabric (Preview)
data.feature_description: Mirror DB integration with Real-Time Intelligence in Microsoft Fabric lets organizations track and process updates to their Mirrored data sources like Azure SQL, Mirroed Snowflake and others. By enabling change feeds on these mirrored datasets, users can quickly build event-driven and AI-powered applications--like dashboards and alerts--without complex setup or deep knowlege of Apache Spark. This streamlined approach delivers instant insights and automation, helping businesses respond to evolving data in real time.
data.feature_name: Enable stream processing and real-time analytics on Mirror DB change feeds

data.blog_title: Announcing Shortcut Transformations: from files to Delta tables. Always in sync, no pipelines required.
data.feature_description: You will soon be able to bring your Excel data directly into Fabric Lakehouse Delta tables without extra steps. This feature allows you to select multiple tabs from a single Excel workbook and load them into structured Delta tables. You can preview and align schemas, automatically handle column mapping, and ensure data types remain consistent. The ingestion process is optimized for speed and reliability. With this update, you can transition from familiar Excel workflows to modern lakehouse architecture effortlessly, making analytics faster and collaboration easier.
data.feature_name: Shortcut Transformations - Excel to Delta

data.feature_description: Currently, users can open multiple workspaces simultaneously and switch between them via the navigation bar. However, the order of these workspace icons is fixed based on the sequence in which they were opened.This creates friction for users who frequently multitask across workspaces and want to organize their open items in a way that better reflects their workflow priorities.This feature allows users to reorder open workspace icons in the navigation bar through drag-and-drop interaction, enabling greater control and efficiency in multitasking.
data.feature_name: Allow users to reorder open workspaces in the nav bar

data.blog_title: Fabric User Data Functions (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: Fabric function support in T-SQL enables users to call external data functions directly from their SQL queries within the Data Warehouse. This allows for advanced processing, enrichment, and transformation while centralizing logic and promoting code reuse across different engines and workloads. By embedding Fabric functions into SQL, teams can streamline operations and maintain consistency across platforms.For example, if you defined a `my_fabric_function` user data function, you cna call it from T-SQL code using the folowing T-SQL syntax:<br/>```SELECT *, my_fabric_function(param1, param2...) FROM table```
data.feature_name: Fabric Functions in DW

data.blog_title: Simplify your data movement with Copy job: CDC with SQL estate (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: Customers can use Copy job to automatically capture inserts, updates, and deletions from any supported CDC source store, and replicate them to the new destination stores including Oracle without requiring a watermark column.
data.feature_name: Copy job - CDC based replication to Oracle

data.blog_title: Native Execution Engine available at no additional cost!
data.feature_description: Enhance the Native Execution Engine in Microsoft Fabric Spark to support CSV ingestion natively, minimizing fallbacks to the Spark JVM and improving performance for data ingestion workflows.Unlock native engine speedups for foundational ingestion scenarios.Reduce cost and latency during ETL, especially for write-heavy delta loads for customers given majority of users  have CSV based file dependencies
data.feature_name: CSV Support for Native Execution Engine

data.blog_title: Fine-grained ReadWrite access to data with OneLake security (Preview)
data.feature_description: Configure write permissions for folders in a lakehouse using OneLake security. With this feature, viewers can be given selective write access to certain folders in the lakehouse enabling teams to write data without having Contributor or higher roles in the workspace.
data.feature_name: OneLake security - ReadWrite permission - GA

data.blog_title: Custom Live Pools for Fabric Data Engineering (Preview)
data.feature_description: Customers can create custom compute pools for Spark with libraries and other items specific to their scenario and keep them warm like they can today with starter pools.
data.feature_name: Custom Live Pools

data.feature_description: Control which tooltips show and how the tooltips display on your visual
data.feature_name: Tooltip options for Power BI visuals

data.blog_title: Creator Improvements in the Data Agent
data.feature_description: The Creator Agent is a specialized AI assistant designed to help data agent creators configure, improve, and optimize their data agents by generating and refining Agent Instructions, Data Source Instructions, and Few-Shot Examples. It addresses common customer pain points such as confusion about where to put instructions, uncertainty about the effectiveness of few shots, and difficulty diagnosing why an agent produces poor results. The agent works in a collaborative, chat-based 'setup' mode, where it analyzes existing configurations, explores database schemas and query patterns, and recommends improvements that users can explicitly accept or reject. It is designed to detect ambiguity and contradictions across configurations and suggest clearer, more consistent alternatives. Initially focused on SQL data sources, the Creator Agent is intended to expand to additional data sources (e.g., KQL, semantic models) over time. Overall, it enables a faster, more scalable, and more understandable way to build high-quality data agents without requiring deep knowledge of the underlying system.
data.feature_name: [PuPr] Assisted Setup Mode in Data Agent

data.blog_title: Capacity Scheduler: Smarter capacity control for Eventhouse (Preview)
data.feature_description: The Eventhouse Min-Capacity Planner helps customers understand and forecast the impact of their Minimum Consumption (Min Capacity) settings on both performance and cost. It models how Eventhouse behaves when a minimum CU level is defined and allows users to schedule different Min-Capacity values per hour of the day and per day of the week. By visualizing baseline consumption, peak-time protection, and off-hours reduction, the planner makes it easy to choose the right minimum levels, avoiding throttling during busy periods while preventing unnecessary consumption during quiet times. It turns Min-Capacity scheduling into a simple, predictable, and actionable planning tool.
data.feature_name: Capacity Planner

data.blog_title: Introducing Eventhouse Endpoint for Fabric Data Warehouse: Real-Time Analytics, Unified Architecture
data.feature_description: The Eventhouse endpoint is a powerful feature in Microsoft Fabric that enables users to query tables with exceptional speed and ease. It allows for real-time insights across your data estate, supporting structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data analysis. This endpoint transforms Fabric into a true real-time analytics platform, combining the simplicity of batch analytics with the responsiveness of event streaming. Additionally, it extends the architecture to structured data sources, allowing users to query Fabric Data Warehouse tables in real-time using KQL. Overall, Eventhouse is designed for managing both streaming and batch data, making it a game-changer for large-scale data operations
data.feature_name: Eventhouse Endpoint for DWH

data.blog_title: Introducing Eventhouse Endpoint for Fabric Data Warehouse: Real-Time Analytics, Unified Architecture
data.feature_description: The Eventhouse endpoint is a powerful feature in Microsoft Fabric that enables users to query tables with exceptional speed and ease. It allows for real-time insights across your data estate, supporting structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data analysis. This endpoint transforms Fabric into a true real-time analytics platform, combining the simplicity of batch analytics with the responsiveness of event streaming. Additionally, it extends the architecture to structured data sources, allowing users to query Fabric Data Warehouse tables in real-time using KQL. Overall, Eventhouse is designed for managing both streaming and batch data, making it a game-changer for large-scale data operations
data.feature_name: Eventhouse Endpoint - for Lakehouse

data.blog_title: Simplify Schema Changes in Fabric Data Warehouse with ALTER COLUMN (Preview)
data.feature_description: This feature enables users to modify the definition of an existing column in a Fabric DW table, specifically allowing changes to the column's data type and size.https://community.fabric.microsoft.com/t5/Fabric-Updates-Blogs/Simplify-Schema-Changes-in-Fabric-Data-Warehouse-with-ALTER/ba-p/5177593
data.feature_name: ALTER COLUMN (Public Preview)

data.feature_description: Ontology item exposes an MCP public endpoint for integration first party and 3rd party custom agents from other platforms.
data.feature_name: [Ontology MCP Endpoint] Initial Query tools

data.blog_title: From legacy to Fabric: A new guided migration experience through Spectral Core Fabric Workload (Preview)
data.feature_description: Spectral Core delivers fast and worry-free migration solution, that makes it easy to move data, metadata and SQL transformations from populate databases and data warehouse to Microsoft Fabric.
data.feature_name: Spectral Core Workload Migration

data.blog_title: The next evolution of OneLake security (Preview)
data.feature_description: OneLake security allows seamless integration with 3rd party query engines through APIs for synchronizing security.
data.feature_name: OneLake security APIs for external engines

data.blog_title: Resource Profiles in Microsoft Fabric Data Engineering (Preview)
data.feature_description: Performance by Default Experience based simple hints from users based on their workload requirementsPre-configured compute and environment settings tailored to specific data engineering workloads based on their requirements and price perf goals from workspace settings
data.feature_name: Resource Profiles for Fabric Data Engineering

data.feature_description: Visibility into Job Concurrency & QueueingWorkspace users and admins can see all active jobs and their states (running, queued, throttled)Diagnose job delays by identifying concurrency limits or queueing bottlenecksCapacity admins can now monitor job activity across all workspaces based on CU load.Understand overall load and capacity pressure
data.feature_name: Job Queueing and Concurrency Monitoring

data.blog_title: OneLake data access roles APIs announcement
data.feature_description: In addition to the existing bulk API for managing OneLake security roles, we will add a new set of APIs for performing granular actions against roles.Examples include: create a single role, add a single member to a role, etc.
data.feature_name: OneLake security granular APIs

data.feature_description: As a data analyst, I want to programmatically manage ontology schemas via a SDK  and query instances via API in so that I can automate and scale downstream analyses.
data.feature_name: Public API for Ontology

data.feature_description: Ontology Integration with AI Foundry as a Knowledge/Context Source.
data.feature_name: [Ontology for Foundry IQ] - Ontology integration with Foundry IQ

data.feature_description: Ontologry interation wtih Agent 365 as 1st party MCP Tool
data.feature_name: [Ontology for A365] - Ontology integration with Agent 365

data.feature_description: **Fabric Chargeback app **The** Microsoft Fabric Chargeback app** helps customers understand which teams, users, and workloads are driving capacity usage, enabling them to build chargeback processes that fairly allocate costs based on actual consumption. As a capacity admin, you can view key parameters such as metric types, dates, and operation names, and share the report with others in your organization to support transparent and informed cost management.  Ultimately, this enables admins to:* Understand cost drivers - See which workspaces, users, or artifacts consume the most resources.* Enable accurate chargeback - Distribute costs fairly across departments based on real usage.* Optimize capacity planning - Identify expensive artifacts and adjust capacity assignments or auto-scale settings to control costs.With these insights, admins can make informed decisions about capacity sizing, workload distribution, and budgeting/chargeback--ensuring cost transparency and operational efficiency.
data.feature_name: Fabric Chargeback app

data.blog_title: Introducing the Item History Page in Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics App (Preview)
data.feature_description: **Fabric Capacity Metrics App - Health **The Health page in the Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics app is currently in Preview, and some minor updates/enhancements will be made to make it Generally Available.  The page offers a high-level overview of all capacities for which a user is admin, aggregating capacity metrics across a tenant in a way that greatly simplifies the ability to identify capacities that are in need of attention. The page is designed to help capacity administrators quickly identify capacities that are consuming the most compute resources or are experiencing issues such as throttling or query rejections. It also provides a clear view of the health status for key metrics, including data refresh history, error details, and connectivity checks. This helps admins quickly identify issues such as failed refreshes or stale data, understand their root causes, and take corrective actions. By ensuring metrics remain accurate and up-to-date, the Health page supports streamlined decision-making visibility to sustain smoother operations.
data.feature_name: Capacity Metrics App - Health

data.blog_title: Introducing the Item History Page in Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics App (Preview)
data.feature_description: **Fabric Capacity Metrics Metrics App -  Timepoint Summary & Item Detail **The **Timepoint Summary** and **Item Detail** pages in the Fabric Capacity Metrics app work together to give you a complete view of your metrics at any specific point in time.The **Timepoint Summary** page provides a 30-second snapshot of interactive and background operations ranked by compute impact, measured in Capacity Units (CUs). Visuals like the heartbeat chart show CU activity over a 60-minute window, making it easy to spot peaks and troughs.  Ultimately, admins can use this page to identify which interactive and background operation types had the highest impact on a capacity, and to then take any appropriate actions to ensure capacity health such as avoiding throttling.Drilling deeper, the **Item Detail** page provides a detailed analysis of the operation type within a specific item.  It lists records contributing to CU usage, with filters like Operation ID, User, and CU threshold to improve accuracy when sampling occurs (e.g., when records exceed 100,000 entries). This level of detail is valuable as, for example, scheduled refresh workflows often trigger multiple internal operations which all contribute to capacity consumption and can be drilled down into through this view.Together, these views help you quickly identify capacity hotspots, optimize workloads, and maintain performance.* **Timepoint Summary Page**  provides a summary view of operation types in items that contribute to capacity usage in the 30-seconds time window, ranked by their compute impact measured in Capacity Units (CUs). Admins can use  it to identify which interactive and background operation types had the highest impact on your capacity.* **Timepoint Item Detail Page** drills deeper into individual metrics at that timepoint, showing detailed values, status, and related context. It allows you to investigate specific metrics, understand their behavior, and confirm accuracy for reporting or decision-making.Together, these pages enable organizations to maintain data consistency, monitor progress, and make informed decisions with confidence by offering both high-level summaries and granular insights.
data.feature_name: Capacity Metrics App - Timepoint Summary & Detail

data.feature_description: Operations Agent can use Ontology as a knowledge source and generate its playbook using entity types, properties, relationships as well as rules & actions definied in the Ontology.
data.feature_name: [Ontology for Operational Agent] - Ontology integration with Operations Agent

data.feature_description: With the increasing integration of artificial intelligence in business tools, Copilots have introduced new challenges in data protection, governance and compliance. This situation demands a refined approach to how AI interacts with and manages organizational data. Purview for AI intends to address these challenges by providing a set of capabilities that enable organizations to monitor, audit, and prevent data loss and risk while their users interact with LLMs from their managed devices.Microsoft Purview enables Copilot and Data Agent to leverage data security, governance, and compliance features in their apps. It provides a smooth integration path to embed data protection capabilities into their workflows, ensuring robust enterprise security, such as:*  Audit: Send prompt and response telemetry along with all associated user and system context to Purview for auditing purposes.*  eDiscovery: Send prompt and response contents, including all associated user and system context, to Purview to support electronic discovery processes.*  Data Lifecycle Management (DLM): Send prompt and response contents with all associated user and system context to Purview to manage the data lifecycle effectively.*  Communications Compliance (CC): Send prompt and response contents, along with all associated user and system context, to Purview to detect and address unethical or improper uses of AI.*  Classification: Send prompt and response contents with all associated user and system context to Purview for classification and store the classification results in compliant storage.
data.feature_name: Data Agent Audit logs with Purview

data.blog_title: Fabric workloads are now generally available!
data.feature_description: **General Availability of Fabric Data Agents**We're preparing to bring the core Fabric Data Agent capabilities to general availability in March. This release will introduce broad support for key data sources, including Lakehouse, Warehouse, Semantic Models, Eventhouse, SQL Databases, and Mirrored Databases. Users will also be able to configure data agent using agent-level instructions, data source-specific instructions, and example queries to tailor behavior to your scenarios. Publishing and sharing within Microsoft Fabric will also be generally available, making it easier to operationalize and collaborate on data agents.In addition, this release includes diagnostic downloads, Git integration, and deployment pipelines as part of Microsoft Fabric's Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) capabilities, enabling robust governance and lifecycle management.
data.feature_name: General Availability of Fabric Data Agents

data.blog_title: Announcing low code Automated ML (AutoML) in Fabric Data Science
data.feature_description: Automated Machine Learning (AutoML) enables users to build and deploy machine learning models by automating the most time-consuming and complex parts of the model development process. The General Availability will enable users to simplofy the ML development cycle by using AutoML to automatically select the best algorithms, tune hyperparameters, and generate optimized models based on the input data and desired outcomes.
data.feature_name: GA AutoML in Fabric

data.blog_title: Dataflow Gen2: Variable Library integration in Microsoft Fabric (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: A new variable type is added to Variable library item, which keeps Cloud connection ID,  enforce permissions and brings better experience for parameterize connections in CI/CD as allowing customize connection based on deployment stage.
data.feature_name: Variable Library - Connection reference variable type

data.blog_title: Introducing New Branching Capabilities in Fabric Git Integration
data.feature_description: Introducing a new Branched workspace is a new developer experience for working with feature workspaces when performing a 'branch-out' operation.Developer will gain more insights with new visual cues understand better which workspaces are related and provide new functionalities which enhance the developer experience when using branch-out functionality.
data.feature_name: Git Integration - Branched Workspaces (Folder Relation)

data.blog_title: Public APIs – bulk import and export items definition (Preview)
data.feature_description: Today, when Importing multiple item definitions, it required multiple API calls, but that requires very deep understanding of how Fabric works in order to make it a successful deployment:- Developer needs to know the order of deployment, based on how items are related to each other.- Developer needs to update the relations of each item, by familiarizing itself with its definition, and swapping the GUIDs of the dependent items.This requires a lot of coding and technical deep dive by developers and its hard to scale. That's why we are building the option to Import multiple items in a single API call. The be benefits of that will be:- a SIngle call to import an subset of items that should be uploaded to a workspace.- Sorting of the order of deployment for that list of items, so users don't need to worry about the order.- In case of LogicalIds are part of the definition of any of the items, the API call will also replace the LogicalId with the proper GUID in the relevant workspace.This should remove a lot of the overhead developers are handling today when trying to build their own custom deployment process in CI/CD.
data.feature_name: REST API - Export and import multiple items in a single call
